content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 16, 1989

R. Oak Dowling, Inc.
Attorney At Law
Courthouse Square
1000 Fourth Street
San Rafael, CA 94901

Re: Your Request for a Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-89-310

Dear Mr. Dowling:

This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
San Rafael City Councilmember Dorothy L. Breiner relative to the
newly enacted provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1/

This letter confirms that your letter dated May 17, 1989 ac-
curately summarizes the telephone advice I provided to you on that
day concerning the decision in Service Employees International
Union, AFL-CIO, et al. v. California Fair Political Practices Com-
mission, United States District Court, Eastern District of
California, Case No. CIVS-89-0433, LKK-JFM (hereafter, "SEIU
case").

As I stated in our telephone conversation, Section 85306
prohibits candidates from using campaign contributions received
prior to January 1, 1989 to support or oppose any candidate for
elective office on or after that date. However, due to a recent
ruling in the SEIU case, funds raised prior to January 1, 1989
that are in compliance with the contribution limits of the Act may
be used for future elections. Funds raised prior to January 1,
1989 that are in excess of the contribution limits of the Act may
be used for any lawful purpose other than to support or oppose a
candidate for elective office.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory refer-
ences are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-89-310
Page 2


     Please be aware that this interpretation is subject to change
once the SEIU case has been decided on the merits.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact this office at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        (By:) John W. Wallace
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JWW:plh

R OAK DOWLING, INC.
ATTORNEY AT LAW
COURTHOUSE SQUARE
SUITE 860
1000 FOURTH STREET
SAN RAFAEL, CALIFORNIA 94901
TELEPHONE (415) 457-6727

May 17, 1989


John Wallace, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE: PROPOSITION 73

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This will confirm our telephone conversation
of May 17, 1989, wherein you advised that under the
decision by United States District Judge Lawrence
Karlton of May 15, 1989, all campaign funds collected
prior to January 1, 1989, may be used for the 1989
elections, subject to contribution limits.

This letter is written in behalf of the
Dorothy L. Breiner campaign for City Council for the
City of San Rafael, 1989 election.

Very truly yours,

R. Oak Dowling

ROD/dmr

     This letter is written in behalf of the

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   R. Oak Dowling

ROD/dmr
cc:  Dorothy L. Breiner, San Rafael City Council

May 23, 1989


R. Oak Dowling
R. Oak Dowling, Inc.
Attorney at Law
Courthouse Square
100 Fourth Street, Suite 580
San Rafael, CA 94901

Re: Letter No. 89-310

Dear Mr. Dowling:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on May 19, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John Wallace an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

428 | Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: Section 85306 prohibits candidates from using campaign contributions received prior to January 1, 1989 to support or oppose any candidate for elective office on or after that date. However, due to a recent ruling in the SEIU case, funds raised prior to January 1, 1989 that are in compliance with the contribution limits of the Act may be used for future elections. Funds raised prior to January 1, 1989 that are in excess of the contribution limits of the Act may be used for any lawful purpose other than to support or oppose a candidate for elective office.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether campaign funds collected prior to January 1, 1989 can be used for future elections and candidate support/opposition activities under section 85306 of the Political Reform Act, in light of the SEIU case ruling.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Compliant pre-January 1, 1989 campaign funds may be used for future elections, while non-compliant excessive funds may only be used for non-candidate purposes. This interpretation is subject to change pending the final SEIU case decision on the merits.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an FPPC response letter (June 16, 1989) confirming prior telephone advice. Also includes the original incoming request letters from May 17, 1989 and an acknowledgment letter from May 23, 1989. The conclusion is derived from the substantive advice provided in the main response letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether campaign funds collected prior to January 1, 1989 can be used for future elections and candidate support/opposition activities under section 85306 of the Political Reform Act, in light of the SEIU case ruling.

CONCLUSION: Section 85306 prohibits candidates from using campaign contributions received prior to January 1, 1989 to support or oppose any candidate for elective office on or after that date. However, due to a recent ruling in the SEIU case, funds raised prior to January 1, 1989 that are in compliance with the contribution limits of the Act may be used for future elections. Funds raised prior to January 1, 1989 that are in excess of the contribution limits of the Act may be used for any lawful purpose other than to support or oppose a candidate for elective office.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice provided to R. Oak Dowling regarding San Rafael Councilmember Dorothy Breiner's campaign regarding the use of pre-January 1, 1989 campaign funds under the Political Reform Act and the SEIU court ruling.
